output:
# NA-HSD-IPM: A Non-Archimedean Homogeneous Self-Dual Interior-Point Method with Unified, Parameter-Free Certificates of Optimality, Infeasibility, and Unboundedness

## 1. Background and Current State of the Art

Primal–dual interior-point methods (IPMs) are the workhorse of modern linear and convex optimization. The canonical reference, Wright's *Primal-Dual Interior-Point Methods* (SIAM, 1997; reference [42] of the source paper), formalizes the central-path-following framework, Newton-system solves, and Mehrotra-type predictor–corrector heuristics (Mehrotra, 1992) that underpin essentially every production LP/QP solver. A persistent practical difficulty in this framework is the diagnosis of **infeasibility** and **unboundedness**: a path-follower assumes a strictly feasible interior point exists, so when the problem is infeasible or unbounded the method must either be augmented with a Big-M penalty (which couples the verdict to a hand-chosen large constant) or embedded into a larger auxiliary system that is guaranteed to be solvable.

The definitive real-field resolution is the **homogeneous self-dual (HSD) embedding** of Ye, Todd, and Mizuno (1994), which constructs a single homogeneous self-dual system in the augmented variables $(x, y, s, \tau, \kappa)$ that is *always* feasible and *always* bounded, admits a trivial strictly-feasible all-ones starting point, requires *no* Big-M, and provably converges in $O(\sqrt{n}\,L)$ iterations. At termination it returns a **Goldman–Tucker** strictly-complementary solution (Goldman & Tucker, 1956), from which a Farkas-type certificate is read off the homogenizing variables: $\tau > 0,\ \kappa = 0 \Rightarrow$ the original problem is solvable (recover $x/\tau$); $\tau = 0,\ \kappa > 0 \Rightarrow$ the problem is primal- or dual-infeasible, with the sign relationship between $c^\top x$ and $b^\top y$ separating primal-infeasibility from dual-infeasibility (i.e., primal-unboundedness). Andersen and Ye (1999) extended this to the monotone complementarity problem, and the textbook treatment of Roos, Terlaky, and Vial (2005) made HSD the standard exposition. The one stubborn practical wrinkle is the **$\tau \approx 0$ ambiguity**: in floating point one must decide whether $\tau$ is *genuinely* positive or merely a small rounding residue, and this decision is governed by an instance-dependent tolerance whose failure modes sharpen as the instance approaches ill-posedness (Renegar, 1995).

In a parallel line of work, Sergeyev's **grossone** methodology and the *Infinity Computer* (Sergeyev, 2017) provide a numerical framework for computing *natively* with actual infinities and infinitesimals over a non-Archimedean field, representing quantities as finite digit-vectors indexed by powers of the grossone ①. For rigorous footing, this algebra can be formalized as a bounded-support truncated Levi-Civita/Hahn series field with explicit total-order and comparison rules and a truncation error model (Shamseddine & Berz, 2010), so that predicates such as "finiteness class" and "leading power" are well-defined algebraic facts rather than implementation conventions. De Cosmis and De Leone (2012) and De Leone (2018) imported grossone into mathematical programming; Cococcioni, Pappalardo, and Sergeyev (2018) built lexicographic multi-objective LP on it; and Cococcioni, Cudazzo, Pappalardo, and Sergeyev (2022) replaced the classical Big-M with a *numerically infinite* $M$. The source paper, **Fiaschi and Cococcioni (2022)**, develops the **NA-IPM**: a primal–dual interior-point method whose arithmetic lives in the non-Archimedean field, computing directly with infinitesimals ($\eta$) and finite/large symbolic magnitudes ($\alpha$). To handle infeasibility and unboundedness, NA-IPM uses a *mild embedding* (their Section 4.1, Eq. 14) that adds just two variables and one constraint to render the problem strictly feasible and bounded. In Experiment 2 (Tables 1–2, Figures 3–4) an unbounded 2D LP (Eq. 22) is embedded into Eq. 23; the method converges finitely, the optimum lands on a hyperplane "infinitely far from the origin," and unboundedness is *detected by ad hoc indicators*: the dual variable $\lambda_3$ (which should be $0$ if bounded but here equals $1$) together with the fact that $x_1, x_2$ emerge as *infinite* numbers. The mirror case (Eqs. 24–25, Fig. 4) is an infeasible primal corresponding to an unbounded dual. Crucially, the embedding penalty weights must be chosen by hand — Proposition 3 recommends $O(\wp_1) = O(\wp_2) = O(\alpha)$ and they pick $\wp_1 = \wp_2 = \alpha$ — a non-Archimedean re-incarnation of the classical Big-M tuning problem. The paper's stated future work points to lexicographic multi-objective semidefinite programming and other families involving infinitesimal/infinite numbers.

## 2. Key Findings and Analysis

The central diagnosis is that the source's machinery for the *optimal-vs-infeasible-vs-unbounded* verdict rests on three coupled crutches: (i) a **problem-specific mild embedding**, (ii) **hand-tuned** infinitesimal/infinite penalty weights $\wp$ (the grossone Big-M), and (iii) **per-instance reading of ad hoc indicators** ($\lambda_3$; the finiteness of individual decision variables). We name this failure mode **Embedding-and-Penalty Dependence (EPD)**: the verdict is not an intrinsic, formulation-independent output of the solver but an artifact of choices the user must make and interpret per instance. EPD is unsatisfying for the same reasons classical Big-M is unsatisfying — the answer can depend on the penalty magnitude, the indicators are not a single uniform certificate, and there is no a priori guarantee that the chosen $\wp$ is large enough yet small enough to avoid masking the true geometry.

We weighed five candidate research directions. **(A) NA-HSD embedding** — port the Ye–Todd–Mizuno homogeneous self-dual construction into the grossone field and read the verdict from the *finiteness class* of $\tau$ and $\kappa$. **(B)** A polynomial-complexity theory for NA-IPM with a grossone bit-length $\tilde{L}$. **(C)** A direct jump to lexicographic multi-objective SDP (the source's future work). **(D)** Numerically stable mixed-magnitude grossone linear algebra / preconditioning of the Newton system. **(E)** A non-Archimedean Farkas/Goldman–Tucker infeasibility-certificate theory. Direction (A) is the strongest choice because it *unifies* (B), (D), and (E) — a convergence proof, a stable mixed-magnitude solve, and a certificate theory are all prerequisites of a working NA-HSD method — while squarely targeting the Introduction's central concern (embeddings and penalty tuning for infeasibility/unboundedness) and *seeding* (C) via self-scaled barriers over symmetric cones.

The decisive design insight is that NA-HSD is a **strict generalization** rather than a competitor. The HSD system is *always* feasible and bounded, so it needs **no $\wp$ / Big-M** (parameter-free) and returns **one** Goldman–Tucker/Farkas certificate (formulation-independent), not per-instance indicators. Moving HSD into the non-Archimedean field then *upgrades* its single weak point: the floating-point "$\tau \approx 0$?" tolerance guess becomes an **exact** test on the *leading grossone power* of $\tau$ — a magnitude is finite-positive, infinitesimal, or infinite as an algebraic fact about its digits, not a numerical judgment call. And the construction is *non-destructive*: projecting the field $\mathbb{E} \to \mathbb{R}$ (sending all infinitesimals to $0$) recovers the **classical real Ye–Todd–Mizuno HSD method exactly**, while the Fiaschi–Cococcioni mild embedding reappears as a particular *non-self-dual* reduction. Both prior methods live inside NA-HSD as corner cases, giving the proposal *falsifiability by construction*.

## 3. Novel Insights & Proposed Research (the 5q)

**High-level insight.** Current non-Archimedean interior-point practice suffers from **Embedding-and-Penalty Dependence (EPD)**: the optimal/infeasible/unbounded verdict is manufactured by a per-instance mild embedding, hand-tuned grossone penalties $\wp$, and ad hoc indicator reading ($\lambda_3$, individual-variable finiteness). We propose **NA-HSD-IPM**, which builds the Ye–Todd–Mizuno homogeneous self-dual embedding *inside* the grossone field $\mathbb{E}$, runs a grossone-arithmetic primal–dual path-follower on a system that is always feasible and always bounded, and reads the trichotomy **exactly (margin-certified)** from the *finiteness class* (finite vs. infinitesimal vs. infinite) of the homogenizing variables $\tau$ and $\kappa$, decoded from their leading grossone powers. We mean *exact* in a precise, conditional sense: the verdict is a discrete finiteness-class decision that is *certified* whenever the leading grossone powers of $\tau$ and $\kappa$ are separated by a margin exceeding the retained truncation depth $d$; absent that separation the method returns an explicit "undecided at depth $d$" rather than guessing. The method is *parameter-free* — in the sense of carrying no user-tuned Big-M / penalty weight $\wp$, while the truncation depth, working precision, and stopping tolerance intrinsic to any IPM remain — *formulation-independent*, and *non-destructive by construction*. It is best read as a unifying formulation that strictly generalizes both prior methods, with the two reductions treated as claims to be *proven/validated, not assumed*: under the limit $\mathbb{E} \to \mathbb{R}$ (all infinitesimals $\to 0$) it should reduce, within the retained digit tolerance, to the classical real Ye–Todd–Mizuno HSD method as we re-implement it (B1), and the source's mild embedding should reappear as a particular non-self-dual reduction (B2) — making the central claim falsifiable by a stated limit rather than asserted.

**[Question 1] - What is the problem?**
Can a homogeneous self-dual embedding of a linear program, constructed over Sergeyev's non-Archimedean grossone field, replace the mild embedding and the hand-tuned grossone Big-M penalties with a single parameter-free, formulation-independent optimality/infeasibility/unboundedness certificate that is reliably decodable from the finiteness class of the homogenizing variables $\tau$ and $\kappa$ under finite-precision grossone arithmetic?

**[Question 2] - Why is it interesting and important?**
Infeasibility and unboundedness detection is not a corner case; it is the daily reality of optimization pipelines (presolve, branch-and-bound nodes, sensitivity analysis, multi-objective scalarization) where many subproblems are ill-posed by design. The real-field community settled this with HSD precisely because Big-M tuning is fragile and instance-dependent; yet the source paper shows the non-Archimedean community has *re-introduced* exactly that fragility through $\wp$-tuning and per-instance indicator reading. Resolving EPD therefore matters on two fronts. First, it gives grossone-based optimization a *single, uniform, certificate-bearing* verdict — the same Goldman–Tucker/Farkas guarantee that makes real HSD trustworthy — rather than a checklist of ad hoc symptoms. Second, it *strengthens* HSD itself: the notorious "$\tau \approx 0$" tolerance decision, which degrades near Renegar's distance-to-ill-posedness, becomes an exact (margin-certified) leading-power magnitude test. This pain is concrete, not rhetorical: we will *exhibit* explicit near-ill-posed witness families, parameterized by Renegar distance, on which real-HSD's tolerance-based $\tau \approx 0$ test demonstrably misclassifies or is acutely tolerance-sensitive — so the contribution is need-driven rather than merely elegant. Because NA-HSD is a unifying formulation that strictly generalizes both the classical real method and the source's mild embedding (each recovered as a corner case, subject to validation), it extends rather than competes; and it directly seeds the source's stated future work — lexicographic multi-objective SDP/SOCP via a non-Archimedean self-scaled (Nesterov–Todd) barrier over symmetric cones — which we scope as future work *outside* the LP core of this proposal.

**[Question 3] - Why is it hard?**
Five compounding difficulties. **(a) Cross-class ill-conditioning:** the Newton/central-path system mixes entries that are finite, infinitesimal, and infinite *simultaneously*, so naive elimination annihilates leading-power structure; one needs grossone-aware block elimination and Nesterov–Todd-style scaling that *preserves* the leading-power algebra across magnitude classes. **(b) Convergence theory:** one must *define* a non-Archimedean central path, a neighborhood/potential function, and then *establish* — as a primary theoretical deliverable-and-risk, conjecture-then-prove for the LP case and explicitly **not inherited** from the source (whose NA-IPM is a proof-of-concept that does not prove polynomial-time convergence) — an $O(\sqrt{n}\,\tilde{L})$ iteration bound, the grossone analog of Ye–Todd–Mizuno self-dual convergence, driving the duality gap down to an *infinitesimal*. **(c) Certificate decidability:** there must be a rigorous threshold theory for when $\tau$'s leading grossone power is "genuinely finite-positive" versus infinitesimal, *and* a guarantee that this finiteness class is reached in *finitely many* iterations (otherwise the exactness claim is vacuous). **(d) Finite-realization error analysis:** grossone is stored as finite digit-vectors over a *bounded* set of powers, and truncation *across* powers can corrupt the certificate; one must bound the number of powers required so that the retained representation cannot flip the verdict. **(e) Conic generalization:** extending to SOCP/SDP needs self-scaled barriers over a non-Archimedean *Jordan algebra*, where even the spectral/eigenvalue machinery must be re-derived for symmetric cones with infinitesimal/infinite eigenvalues.

**[Question 4] - Why hasn't it been solved before?**
The two ingredients lived in separate communities and neither side had reason to cross. The HSD embedding matured inside real-field interior-point research (Ye–Todd–Mizuno 1994; Andersen–Ye 1999; Roos–Terlaky–Vial 2005), where the $\tau \approx 0$ tolerance was an accepted nuisance, not a motivating defect — there was no exact arithmetic to make the magnitude test crisp, so no incentive to seek one. Conversely, the grossone-optimization community arrived at infeasibility/unboundedness through Big-M-style penalties (Cococcioni et al., 2022) and the mild embedding (Fiaschi–Cococcioni, 2022), naturally reaching for penalty constructions because that is the idiom of the field; HSD was simply not on their radar as the canonical alternative. Building NA-HSD requires *both* a working non-Archimedean primal–dual path-follower (only recently available) *and* a willingness to re-derive HSD's self-dual convergence and Goldman–Tucker certificate over a field where the homogenizing variables can be *legitimately* infinitesimal or infinite — which changes the meaning of the certificate from a tolerance test to a finiteness-class decision. No prior work has constructed HSD in a grossone field, so the unifying observation — that the field's *exactness* is precisely what cures HSD's one weakness while HSD's *self-duality* is precisely what cures EPD — had not been made.

**[Question 5] - What are the key components of my approach and results?**
*Method:* (1) derive the **NA-HSD model** — the homogeneous self-dual system in $(x, y, s, \tau, \kappa)$ over the field $\mathbb{E}$, specified over a formally-defined bounded-support algebra (a truncated Levi-Civita/Hahn series field with explicit total-order/comparison rules and a truncation error model; Shamseddine & Berz, 2010) so that "finiteness class" and "leading power" are well-defined algebraic predicates, not implementation conventions; (2) define a **non-Archimedean central path** with a potential/neighborhood; (3) build a **grossone-aware Newton solve** with Nesterov–Todd self-scaled scaling that preserves leading-power structure, wrapped in a **Mehrotra-type predictor–corrector** adapted to grossone arithmetic; (4) specify the **certificate-extraction rule** reading the trichotomy from the leading grossone powers of $\tau$ and $\kappa$; (5) *establish*, as a primary theoretical deliverable-and-risk (conjecture-then-prove for the LP case, not assumed), an $O(\sqrt{n}\,\tilde{L})$ iteration bound *and* finite convergence to the correct finiteness class. *Realization:* implement on the Sergeyev Infinity Computer arithmetic, or simulate grossone as truncated Levi-Civita/Hahn power series of bounded support (Shamseddine & Berz, 2010). *Benchmarks:* Netlib LP; the Netlib/Mészáros infeasible sets; Mittelmann unbounded instances; a synthetic generator parameterized by Renegar distance-to-ill-posedness; lexicographic MO-LP/QP benches (Cococcioni–Pappalardo–Sergeyev 2018; Fiaschi–Cococcioni 2022); and — labeled explicitly as an **exploratory prototype outside the LP core** (consistent with the LP-focused Q1) — small SDPLIB infeasible instances for a conic sketch, with the Nesterov–Todd conic generalization left to future work. *Baselines:* classical real HSD IPM (Mosek/SeDuMi/ECOS with tolerance-based $\tau \approx 0$); modern real-HSD with aggressive presolve/postsolve; a facial-reduction / certificate-purification baseline; an exact/rational LP solver on small instances as independent ground truth; the Fiaschi–Cococcioni NA mild-embedding IPM with tuned $\wp = \alpha$; and Big-M with a numerically infinite $M$ (Cococcioni et al., 2022). *Metrics:* trichotomy detection accuracy; false-verdict rate near the feasibility boundary versus Renegar condition number (computed on the controlled **synthetic families only**, since Renegar distance is expensive/approximate on real benchmarks); iteration counts versus $n$; certificate residual (Farkas/Goldman–Tucker) and its independent verifiability in rational arithmetic; **invariance to $\wp$** (the headline); number of grossone-powers needed for correctness and truncation-depth sensitivity; finite-precision stability of the leading powers; presolve interaction; and memory/runtime overhead versus number of grossone terms. *Falsifiability spine:* **(B1) projection collapse** — under $\mathbb{E} \to \mathbb{R}$ the solver must reproduce our re-implemented real Ye–Todd–Mizuno HSD trajectory within the retained digit tolerance; **(B2) reduction-equivalence** — configured as a non-self-dual reduction it must reproduce the source's Tables 1–2 (including $\lambda_3 = 1$ and infinite $x_1, x_2$) on Eqs. 22–25; **(B3) monotonicity sweep** — verdict correctness versus grossone-digit budget and condition number, checked against an *exact-rational-arithmetic oracle* reported strictly as an **upper bound**; **(B4) invariance placebo** — sweeping $\wp$ across magnitudes, classical Big-M and the mild embedding must flip their verdicts while NA-HSD's verdict stays **invariant** (it has no $\wp$). *Expected outcome:* a parameter-free, correct optimal/infeasible/unbounded trichotomy with exact (margin-certified) certificates where Big-M and the mild embedding require tuning and where real HSD suffers the $\tau \approx 0$ ambiguity, at a *bounded* (small-constant number of grossone-powers) arithmetic overhead, with an $O(\sqrt{n}\,\tilde{L})$ iteration bound *conjectured and proven for the LP case — a central theoretical risk, not an assumption* — matching classical HSD up to the non-Archimedean bit-length. *Honest limits:* margin-certified exactness holds only up to the retained grossone digits — a finite truncation can in principle be defeated by an adversarially tiny Renegar gap, in which case the method declares "undecided at depth $d$"; the conjectured complexity bound carries the usual self-dual caveats and remains the headline theoretical risk; and the conic extension is an exploratory prototype, not a complete theory. *Responsible-research note:* dual-use risk is low — this is core numerical-optimization infrastructure; the principal risks are (i) *over-claiming exactness beyond the digits retained*, mitigated by the margin-certified/"undecided" discipline, an open digit-budget report, and a fully specified truncated-series simulator; (ii) *dependence on community acceptance of nonstandard grossone arithmetic* — a documented skeptical view exists (De Cosmis & De Leone, 2012), which we address by emitting Farkas/Goldman–Tucker certificates that are *independently checkable in rational arithmetic*, so correctness does not rest on accepting the grossone framework; and (iii) the practical advantage over mature real-HSD solvers must be *demonstrated, not assumed*, which the presolve-equipped and facial-reduction baselines are designed to test.

## 4. References and Evidence

1. Y. Ye, M. J. Todd, S. Mizuno (1994). An $O(\sqrt{n}L)$-iteration homogeneous and self-dual linear programming algorithm. *Mathematics of Operations Research* 19(1):53–67. DOI:10.1287/moor.19.1.53.
2. A. J. Goldman, A. W. Tucker (1956). Theory of linear programming. In *Linear Inequalities and Related Systems*, Annals of Mathematics Studies 38, Princeton University Press, pp. 53–97.
3. S. J. Wright (1997). *Primal-Dual Interior-Point Methods.* SIAM, Philadelphia. DOI:10.1137/1.9781611971453. [reference 42 of the source]
4. C. Roos, T. Terlaky, J.-Ph. Vial (2005). *Interior Point Methods for Linear Optimization.* Springer. DOI:10.1007/b101720.
5. E. D. Andersen, Y. Ye (1999). On a homogeneous algorithm for the monotone complementarity problem. *Mathematical Programming* 84:375–399.
6. Yu. Nesterov, M. J. Todd (1997). Self-scaled barriers and interior-point methods for convex programming. *Mathematics of Operations Research* 22(1):1–42. DOI:10.1287/moor.22.1.1.
7. J. F. Sturm (1999). Using SeDuMi 1.02, a MATLAB toolbox for optimization over symmetric cones. *Optimization Methods and Software* 11–12:625–653. DOI:10.1080/10556789908805766.
8. S. Mehrotra (1992). On the implementation of a primal-dual interior point method. *SIAM Journal on Optimization* 2(4):575–601. DOI:10.1137/0802028.
9. J. Renegar (1995). Incorporating condition measures into the complexity theory of linear programming. *SIAM Journal on Optimization* 5(3):506–524. DOI:10.1137/0805037.
10. L. Fiaschi, M. Cococcioni (2022). A Non-Archimedean Interior Point Method and Its Application to the Lexicographic Multi-Objective Quadratic Programming. *Mathematics* 10(23):4536. DOI:10.3390/math10234536; arXiv:2110.15658. **[SOURCE]**
11. M. Cococcioni, A. Cudazzo, M. Pappalardo, Y. D. Sergeyev (2022). The Big-M method with the numerical infinite M. *Optimization Letters* 16:413–431. DOI:10.1007/s11590-021-01715-7.
12. M. Cococcioni, M. Pappalardo, Y. D. Sergeyev (2018). Lexicographic multi-objective linear programming using grossone methodology: Theory and algorithm. *Applied Mathematics and Computation* 318:298–311. DOI:10.1016/j.amc.2017.06.044.
13. Y. D. Sergeyev (2017). Numerical infinities and infinitesimals: Methodology, applications, and repercussions on two Hilbert problems. *EMS Surveys in Mathematical Sciences* 4(2):219–320. DOI:10.4171/EMSS/4-2-3.
14. S. De Cosmis, R. De Leone (2012). The use of grossone in mathematical programming and operations research. *Applied Mathematics and Computation* 218(16):8029–8038. DOI:10.1016/j.amc.2012.02.078. (a critical/skeptical assessment of grossone in OR)
15. R. De Leone (2018). Nonlinear programming and grossone: Quadratic programming and the role of constraint qualifications. *Applied Mathematics and Computation* 318:290–297. DOI:10.1016/j.amc.2017.03.029.
16. K. Shamseddine, M. Berz (2010). Analysis on the Levi-Civita field, a brief overview. *Contemporary Mathematics* 508:215–237. DOI:10.1090/conm/508/10002.
17. F. A. Potra, S. J. Wright (2000). Interior-point methods. *Journal of Computational and Applied Mathematics* 124(1–2):281–302. DOI:10.1016/S0377-0427(00)00433-7.
18. R. M. Freund (1991). Theoretical efficiency of a shifted-barrier-function algorithm for linear programming. *Linear Algebra and Its Applications* 152:19–41. DOI:10.1016/0024-3795(91)90264-Y.
19. R. D. C. Monteiro, I. Adler (1989). Interior path following primal-dual algorithms. Part I: Linear programming. *Mathematical Programming* 44:27–41. DOI:10.1007/BF01587075.
20. R. De Leone, N. Egidi, L. Fatone (2020). The use of grossone in elastic net regularization and sparse support vector machines. *Soft Computing* 24(23):17669–17677. DOI:10.1007/s00500-020-05127-8.

**Primary evidence chain.** The argument proceeds from the source Introduction (Fiaschi & Cococcioni, 2022, ref. 10 — mild embedding, hand-tuned $\wp$, $\lambda_3$/infinite-variable indicators) → classical HSD theory (Ye–Todd–Mizuno 1994, ref. 1; Goldman–Tucker 1956, ref. 2; Andersen–Ye 1999, ref. 5) which removes Big-M and yields a uniform certificate → grossone arithmetic (Sergeyev 2017, ref. 13; Shamseddine–Berz 2010, ref. 16) which renders the certificate's $\tau$-magnitude test *exact (margin-certified)* rather than tolerance-based, with Nesterov–Todd self-scaling (ref. 6) seeding the conic extension toward the source's stated future work.
